title: CSE 160 — Computer Graphics (UCSC, Winter 2021)
description: WebGL assignments and LaTeX homework writeups from Sri Kurniawan's intro graphics course. Three interactive demos covering 2D drawing primitives, shape transformations, and a fully-articulated 3D Charmeleon, plus four LaTeX writeups covering the math (linear algebra, transformations, color theory, lighting + viewing).

assets.title: Asg 0 — 2D Drawing
assets.description: Click-and-drag 2D primitives on a canvas with WebGL. Sets up the rendering pipeline, gl-matrix transforms, and basic event-driven drawing.

assets.title: Asg 1 — Shapes & Transformations
assets.description: Interactive shapes with transformation matrix demonstrations — translate, rotate, scale a hierarchy of triangles + squares using gl-matrix.

assets.title: Asg 2 — Derpy Charmeleon (3D Articulated Character)
assets.description: Fully-articulated 3D Charmeleon. Drag to rotate camera, scroll to zoom, slide controls to pose the limbs and waggle the tail. Hierarchy of joints with parent-relative transforms, animation toggle.

assets.title: HW1 — Linear Algebra
assets.description: LaTeX writeup: vectors, matrix multiplication, dot/cross products, change of basis.

assets.title: HW2 — Modeling & Transformations
assets.description: LaTeX writeup: 2D/3D transformation matrices, homogeneous coordinates, transformation composition.

assets.title: HW3 — Colors & Textures
assets.description: LaTeX writeup: RGB/HSV color spaces, texture mapping, filtering (nearest/bilinear/mipmap).

assets.title: HW4 — Lighting & Viewing
assets.description: LaTeX writeup: Phong/Blinn-Phong shading, perspective vs orthographic projection, frustum culling.
